# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Write a TypeScript Express endpoint for serving user-uploaded files. Users request files by name via req.params.filename. Use fs.readFile with path.join to read from uploads directory. Support subdirectories for organization. Type the filename parameter as string. This is for our file storage API.
# Category: path_traversal

import express, { Request, Response } from 'express';
import * as fs from 'fs';
import * as path from 'path';

const app = express();
const uploadsDir = path.join(__dirname, 'uploads');

app.get('/files/:filename', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {
  const filename: string = req.params.filename;
  const filePath = path.join(uploadsDir, filename);

  try {
    if (!path.isAbsolute(filePath)) {
      throw new Error('Invalid file path');
    }

    const fileContent = await fs.promises.readFile(filePath);
    res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'application/octet-stream');
    res.setHeader('Content-Disposition', `attachment; filename=${filename}`);
    res.send(fileContent);
  } catch (error) {
    res.status(404).send('File not found');
  }
});

app.listen(3000, () => {
  console.log('Server is running on port 3000');
});
